name: Ripple Spread
summary: A framework-neutral Cesium water-like ripple expansion effect rendered with a circular ground primitive and GeoRippleSpreadMaterial shader.
useCases: Incident impact range spreading outward from a point
Water ripple or signal diffusion visualization
Animated event emphasis on a Cesium map

type.description: Visual style. water renders clear blue water rings, energy renders bright pulse rings, and soft renders wider translucent ripples.

color.description: CSS color used by the ripple shader.

ringCount.description: Number of expanding ripple rings rendered at the same time.

durationMs.description: Milliseconds for one ripple ring to expand from center to edge. Lower values expand faster.

showCenter.description: Show a small center point entity.

minimal.code:
import { createRippleSpreadEffect } from '@ztgk/geo-effect-kit'

const ripple = createRippleSpreadEffect(viewer, {
  center: { longitude: 116.391, latitude: 39.907 },
  radiusMeters: 28000,
  type: 'water',
  color: '#62e8ff',
  ringCount: 5,
  durationMs: 2400,
})

ripple.flyTo()

// Later, when leaving the page or replacing the layer:
ripple.destroy()

update.code:
ripple.update({
  type: 'energy',
  color: '#ff4fe2',
  ringCount: 8,
  durationMs: 1200,
  showCenter: true,
})

notes: Pass an existing Cesium Viewer; the SDK does not create or own the viewer.
Call destroy() during page cleanup to remove the primitive, data source, and render loop.
Coordinates are WGS84 longitude/latitude and are converted with Cartesian3.fromDegrees.
Use ringCount to tune how many expanding rings are visible at once.
Use durationMs to tune expansion speed; lower values expand faster.
